source.title: Play, games, and the development of collective intentionality
source.venue: New Directions for Child and Adolescent Development

confidence_rationale: Theoretical review paper -- not primary empirical data, but represents the leading developmental theory on games and collective intentionality. The key claim maps directly to MECH-197 and ARC-049. Source quality is strong (leading researcher in the field, peer-reviewed). Mapping fidelity is high for the conceptual claims. Transfer risk is moderate: the paper theorizes about human child development and the claim maps to REE architecture via the play type progression thesis.
failure_signatures: Review paper without primary empirical data; theoretical claims require separate empirical support.
Does not distinguish games-with-rules from cooperative play at the architectural level -- progression into multi-agent full bilateral frame is not mapped.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Play and games are the developmental crucible for collective intentionality: children first enter conventional, rule-governed practices through play, progressing from individual pretense through shared pretense to games with externally enforceable, agent-neutral rules. Games require understanding that rules are constitutive -- not merely regulative -- and apply to any participant in the practice.
mapping.ree_translation: This directly evidences MECH-197's play type progression claim. Sensorimotor and constructive play train E1/E2; pretend play exercises MECH-094 + ARC-049 intersection; games with rules introduce the constitutive rule layer that requires structured shared-state monitoring (the ARC-049 frame becomes a structured constraint set, not just authorization). The progression is driven by increasing demands on collective intentionality infrastructure, matching REE's layered claim.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper describes the progression in terms of collective intentionality development, not neural architecture. REE's mapping of play types to specific subsystems (E1/E2/E3/hippocampus) is not directly supported -- the paper provides the developmental-psychological layer but not the mechanism.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review of developmental psychology literature on play and games. Human child data.

confidence_components.notes: Strong theoretical anchor but transfer from conceptual framework to REE subsystem architecture requires interpretive step.
